[[Beverly Hills, California]], U.S. | host = [[Jerrod Carmichael]] | best_film_drama = ''[[The Fabelmans]]'' | best_film_musical_or_comedy = ''[[The Banshees of Inisherin]]'' | best_drama_series = ''[[House of the Dragon]]'' | best_musical_or_comedy_series = ''[[Abbott Elementary]]'' | best_miniseries_or_tv_movie = ''[[The White Lotus]]'' | most_wins = ''[[Abbott Elementary]]''
''[[The Banshees of Inisherin]]'' (3) | most_nominations = {{nowrap|''[[The Banshees of Inisherin]]'' (8)}} | network = [[NBC]]
[[Peacock (streaming service)|Peacock]] (streaming) | ratings = 6.3 million ([[Nielsen Media Research#Nielsen TV ratings|Nielsen ratings]]){{cite web |url=https://variety.com/2023/tv/news/golden-globes-ratings-2023-nbc-1235482220/ |title=Golden Globes Ratings Down More Than 26% from Last NBC Show in 2021 |first=Jennifer |last=Maas |work=[[Variety (magazine)|Variety]] |date=January 11, 2023 |access-date=January 13, 2023 |archive-date=January 13, 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230113052505/https://variety.com/2023/tv/news/golden-globes-ratings-2023-nbc-1235482220/ |url-status=live}} | last = [[79th Golden Globes|79th]] | next = [[81st Golden Globes|81st]] }} The '''80th Golden Globe Awards''' honored the best in [[2022 in film|film]] and [[2022 in American television|American television of 2022]], as chosen by the [[Hollywood Foreign Press Association]] (HFPA). The ceremony was held on January 10, 2023, from [[The Beverly Hilton]] in [[Beverly Hills, California]], produced by [[Dick Clark Productions]], Jesse Collins Entertainment, and the HFPA. It aired live in the United States on [[NBC]] and streamed on [[Peacock (streaming service)|Peacock]].{{cite web |url=https://variety.com/2022/tv/news/golden-globes-2023-nbc-hfpa-dick-clark-productions-1235324403/ |title=Golden Globes Return to TV in 2023, NBC and HFPA Set One-Year Deal |first=Michael ceremony.{{cite web |url=https://variety.com/2022/tv/news/golden-globes-host-jerrod-carmichael-nbc-2023-1235453788/ |title=Jerrod Carmichael Named Host of the Golden Globe Awards (Exclusive) ceremony to air on NBC before the HFPA was rebranded as the [[Golden Globe Foundation]] on June 12, 2023.{{cite web |url=https://deadline.com/2023/06/golden-globes-acquired-dick-clark-productions-eldridge-hfpa-shut-down-1235414600/ |title=Golden Globes Acquired by Dick Clark Productions & Eldridge; HFPA to Wind Down |first=Nellie |last=Andreeva |work=[[Deadline Hollywood]] |date=June 12, 2023 |access-date=November 22, 2024}} The nominees were announced on December 12, 2022.{{cite web |url=https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/tv/tv-news/how-to-watch-2023-golden-globe-awards-nominations-1235279534/ |title=Golden Globe Nominations: How to Watch |first=Carly |last=Thomas |work=[[The Hollywood Reporter]] |date=December 11, 2022 |access-date=December 11, 2022 |archive-date=December 12, 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20221212052125/https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/tv/tv-news/how-to-watch-2023-golden-globe-awards-nominations-1235279534/ |url-status=live}}{{cite web |url=https://deadline.com/2022/12/2023-golden-globe-nominations-list-movies-tv-1235196396/ |title=Golden Globe Nominations: The Complete List |first1=Patrick |last1=Hipes |first2=Denise |last2=Petski |work=[[Deadline Hollywood]] |date=December 12, 2022 |access-date=December 12, 2022 |archive-date=December 15, 2022 Lopez|George]] and Mayan Lopez were scheduled to announce the nominations together, but the former dropped out after testing positive for [[COVID-19]]; he was replaced by [[Selenis Leyva]].{{cite web |url=https://variety.com/2022/tv/news/george-mayan-lopez-announce-golden-globes-2023-nominations-1235450721/ |title=NBC Sitcom Stars George Lopez and Mayan Lopez Will Announce This Year's Golden |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20221209193739/https://variety.com/2022/tv/news/george-mayan-lopez-announce-golden-globes-2023-nominations-1235450721/ |url-status=live}}{{cite magazine |url=https://people.com/movies/george-lopez-drops-out-of-revealing-golden-globe-2023-nominees-after-he-contracts-covid/ |title=George Lopez Drops Out of Announcing Golden Globe 2023 Nominations After He Tests Positive for COVID |first=Tommy |last=McArdle |magazine=[[People (magazine)|People]] |date=December 12, 2022 |access-date=December 12, 2022 |archive-date=January 11, 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230111145420/https://people.com/movies/george-lopez-drops-out-of-revealing-golden-globe-2023-nominees-after-he-contracts-covid/ |url-status=live}}{{cite magazine |url=https://ew.com/awards/golden-globes/george-lopez-misses-golden-globes-nominations-selenis-leyva/ |title=George Lopez misses Golden Globes nominations after COVID diagnosis, Selenis Leyva fills in |first=Joey |last=Nolfi |magazine=[[Entertainment Weekly]] |date=December 12, 2022 |access-date=December 12, 2022 |archive-date=January 11, 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230111145413/https://ew.com/awards/golden-globes/george-lopez-misses-golden-globes-nominations-selenis-leyva/ |url-status=live}} [[Eddie Murphy]] and [[Ryan Murphy (producer)|Ryan Murphy]] were announced as the recipients of the [[Golden Globe Cecil B. DeMille Award|Cecil B. DeMille Award]] and [[Golden Globe Carol Burnett Award|Carol Burnett Award]], respectively.{{cite web |url=https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/movies/movie-news/golden-globes-eddie-murphy-cecil-b-demille-award-1235281595/ |title=Golden Globes: Eddie Murphy to Receive Cecil B. DeMille Award (Exclusive) |first=Chris |last=Gardner |work=[[The Hollywood Reporter]] |date=December 14, 2022 |access-date=December 15, 2022 |archive-date=December 14, 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20221214224903/https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/movies/movie-news/golden-globes-eddie-murphy-cecil-b-demille-award-1235281595/ |url-status=live}}{{cite web |url=https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/tv/tv-news/golden-globes-ryan-murphy-carol-burnett-award-1235282418/ |title=Golden Globes: Ryan Murphy to Receive Carol Burnett Award for TV Career Achievements |first=Chris |last=Gardner |work=[[The Hollywood Reporter]] |date=December 15, 2022 |access-date=December 16, 2022 |archive-date=December 16, 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20221216031307/https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/tv/tv-news/golden-globes-ryan-murphy-carol-burnett-award-1235282418/ |url-status=live}} ''[[The Banshees of Inisherin]]'' received a leading 8 nominations, including [[Golden Globe Award for Best Motion Picture – Musical or Comedy|Best Motion Picture – Musical or Comedy]] and [[Golden Globe Award for Best Director|Best Director]] ([[Martin McDonagh]]), the most achieved by any film since ''[[Cold Mountain (film)|Cold Mountain]]'' in [[61st Golden Globe Awards|2004]]; it also, alongside [[American Broadcasting Company|ABC]]'s first-year [[mockumentary]] ''[[Abbott Elementary]]'', won the most awards at the ceremony with three wins.{{cite web |url=https://news.yahoo.com/golden-globes-unveil-nominations-censured-012324712.html |title='Banshees of Inisherin' tops nominations as Golden Globes attempt comeback |first=Andrew |last=Marszal |publisher=[[Yahoo! News]] |date=December 11, 2022 |access-date=December 11, 2022 |archive-date=December 29, 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20221229172730/https://news.yahoo.com/golden-globes-unveil-nominations-censured-012324712.html |url-status=dead}}{{cite web |url=https://www.forbes.com/sites/marisadellatto/2023/01/10/golden-globes-2023-abbott-elementary-the-banshees-of-inisherin-win-big/ |title=Golden Globes 2023: 'Abbott Elementary', 'The Banshees of Inisherin' Win Big |first=Marisa |last=Dellatto |work=[[Forbes]] |date=January 10, 2023 |access-date=January 13, 2023 |archive-date=February 20, 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230220094921/https://www.forbes.com/sites/marisadellatto/2023/01/10/golden-globes-2023-abbott-elementary-the-banshees-of-inisherin-win-big/ |url-status=live}} The ceremony also marked [[Lisa Marie Presley]]'s last public appearance before her death two days later on January 12.{{cite magazine |url=https://ew.com/awards/golden-globes/lisa-marie-presley-last-public-appearance-support-elvis/ |title=Lisa Marie Presley's last public appearance was in support of 'Elvis' |first=Lauren |last=Huff |magazine=[[Entertainment Weekly]] |date=January 12, 2023 |access-date=January 13, 2023 |archive-date=January 13, 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230113032217/https://ew.com/awards/golden-globes/lisa-marie-presley-last-public-appearance-support-elvis/ |url-status=live}} ==Ceremony information== On September 20, 2022, the HFPA and Golden Globe Awards producer [[Dick Clark Productions]] announced that the ceremony would be broadcast on [[NBC]] and streamed on [[Peacock (streaming service)|Peacock]] under a one-year contract. After being the Golden Globes' regular broadcaster since 1996, NBC declined to air the [[79th Golden Globe Awards|previous year's ceremony]] in support of [[boycott]]s by various media companies, actors, and other creatives over the HFPA's history of financial malfeasance and lack of action to increase the membership [[Diversity (business)|diversity]] of the organization.{{cite web |url=https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/news/general-news/golden-globes-returning-nbc-2023-1235166872/ |title=NBC to Bring Back the Golden Globes (Exclusive) |first=Scott |last=Fienberg |work=[[The Hollywood Reporter]] |date=August 9, 2022 |access-date=September 20, 2022 |archive-date=September 21, 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220921030406/https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/news/general-news/golden-globes-returning-nbc-2023-1235166872/ |url-status=live}}{{cite web |url=https://deadline.com/2022/09/golden-globes-nbc-return-2023-hfpa-1235122811/ |title=Golden Globes Returning to NBC in 2023 on One-Year Deal |first=Erik |last=Pedersen |work=[[Deadline Hollywood]] |date=September 20, 2022 |access-date=September 20, 2022 |archive-date=September 20, 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220920165126/https://deadline.com/2022/09/golden-globes-nbc-return-2023-hfpa-1235122811/ |url-status=live}}{{cite web |url=https://deadline.com/2022/12/golden-globes-2023-hfpa-distrust-diversity-1235194362/ |title=Golden Globes Return to NBC Still Tainted by Tinseltown's Distrust of HFPA |first1=Lynette |last1=Rice |first2=Dominic |last2=Patten |first3=Matt |last3=Grobar |work=[[Deadline Hollywood]] |date=December 9, 2022 |access-date=December 11, 2022 |archive-date=January 11, 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230111145435/https://deadline.com/2022/12/golden-globes-2023-hfpa-distrust-diversity-1235194362/ |url-status=live}} The HFPA had previously approved a major [[restructuring]] in July 2022, under which HFPA interim CEO [[Todd Boehly]] agreed to establish a [[For-profit corporation|for-profit entity]] via his holding company [[Eldridge Industries]] (owner of DCP and entertainment trade publication ''[[The Hollywood Reporter]]'') that will hold the Golden Globe Awards' [[intellectual property]] and oversee the "professionalization and modernization" of the ceremony, including "[increasing] the size and diversity of the available voters for the annual awards". The HFPA's philanthropic activities would continue separately as a non-profit entity.{{cite web |url=https://deadline.com/2022/07/hfpa-will-be-both-a-private-company-non-profit-1235080814/ |title=HFPA Will Be Both a Private Company & Non-Profit |first=Anthony |last=D'Alessandro |work=[[Deadline Hollywood]] |date=July 28, 2022 |access-date=August 5, 2022 |archive-date=August 4, 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220804125233/https://deadline.com/2022/07/hfpa-will-be-both-a-private-company-non-profit-1235080814/ |url-status=live}}{{cite web |url=https://variety.com/2022/tv/news/golden-globes-eldridge-hfpa-1235328891/ |title=In Move to Save Golden Globes, HFPA to Become For-Profit Org, Add More Voting Members |first=Michael |last=Schneider |work=[[Variety (magazine)|Variety]] |date=July 28, 2022 |access-date=August 5, 2022 |archive-date=August 4, 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220804131412/https://variety.com/2022/tv/news/golden-globes-eldridge-hfpa-1235328891/ |url-status=live}}{{cite web |url=https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/movies/movie-features/golden-globes-group-head-interview-hfpa-boycott-awards-1235276198/ |title='This is Not the Old HFPA': Golden Globes' Group Head Talks for First Time Since 2021 Boycott |first=Rebecca |last=Keegan |work=[[The Hollywood Reporter]] |date=December 8, 2022 |access-date=December 11, 2022 |archive-date=January 11, 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230111145414/https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/movies/movie-features/golden-globes-group-head-interview-hfpa-boycott-awards-1235276198/ |url-status=live}} Due to conflicts with NBC's ''[[NBC Sunday Night Football|Sunday Night Football]]'' (the [[National Football League|NFL]] regular season has been extended with an additional game since 2021), and to avoid competing with the [[2023 College Football Playoff National Championship]] on Monday January 9 (a game played at [[Inglewood, California|Inglewood]]'s [[SoFi Stadium]]) and the [[28th Critics' Choice Awards]] the following Sunday (January 15), the Globes ceremony was scheduled for Tuesday, January 10, 2023. It was the first Golden Globes ceremony to take place on a Tuesday since [[19th Golden Globe Awards|the 19th edition]] in 1962, as well as the first to be staged on a weeknight since the [[64th Golden Globe Awards|64th Golden Globes]] were presented on Monday, January 15, 2007.{{cite web |url=https://www.thewrap.com/2023-golden-globes-predictions-nbc-show-broadcast/ |title=TheWrap Predicts the 2023 Golden Globes Winners – and Whether NBC Will Cut Ties After the Show |first=Steve |last=Pond |work=[[TheWrap]] |date=January 9, 2023 |access-date=January 10, 2023 |archive-date=January 11, 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230111145425/https://www.thewrap.com/2023-golden-globes-predictions-nbc-show-broadcast/ |url-status=live}} Among the changes include modifications to the supporting acting categories for Television: Both categories for [[Golden Globe Award for Best Supporting Actor – Series, Miniseries or Television Film|Best Supporting Actor]] and [[Golden Globe Award for Best Supporting Actress – Series, Miniseries or Television Film|Best Supporting Actress in a Series, Miniseries, or Television Film]] have been split into separate categories for "Musical-Comedy or Drama" and "Limited Series, Anthology Series, or Motion Picture Made for Television".{{cite press release |url=https://goldenglobes.com/articles/the-hfpa-announces-awards-timetable-for-the-80th-annual-golden-globe-awards/ |title=The HFPA Announces Awards Timetable for the 80th Annual Golden Globe Awards |publisher=[[Golden Globe Awards|Golden Globes]] |date=September 28, 2022 |access-date=November 25, 2024 |archive-date=June 22, 2024 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240622201617/https://goldenglobes.com/articles/the-hfpa-announces-awards-timetable-for-the-80th-annual-golden-globe-awards/ |url-status=live}}{{cite web |url=https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/tv/tv-news/hfpa-new-tv-acting-categories-2023-golden-globes-1235229175/ |title=HFPA Adds New TV Acting Categories for 2023 Golden Globes |first=Beatrice |last=Verhoeven |work=[[The Hollywood Reporter]] |date=September 28, 2022 |access-date=September 30, 2022 |archive-date=January 11, 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230111145416/https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/tv/tv-news/hfpa-new-tv-acting-categories-2023-golden-globes-1235229175/ |url-status=live}}{{cite web |url=https://variety.com/2022/film/news/golden-globes-supporting-tv-categories-awards-calendar-1235386871/ |title=Golden Globes Announce New TV Categories and Awards Timeline |first=Clayton |last=Davis |work=[[Variety (magazine)|Variety]] |date=September 28, 2022 |access-date=September 30, 2022 |archive-date=October 3, 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20221003122536/https://variety.com/2022/film/news/golden-globes-supporting-tv-categories-awards-calendar-1235386871/ |url-status=live}} However, it was announced on September 26, 2023, that both categories have been eliminated in order to make room for two new categories: "Cinematic and Box Office Achievement" and "Best Performance in Stand-Up Comedy on Television" (also known as "Best Stand-Up Comedian on Television"); both eliminated categories will now be remerged into one each, but retitled as "Best Performance by a Male Actor in a Supporting Role on Television" (or "Best Supporting Male Actor – Television") and "Best Performance by a Female Actor in a Supporting Role on Television" (or "Best Supporting Female Actor – Television"), respectively.{{cite web |url=https://variety.com/2023/film/news/golden-globes-adds-new-categories-box-office-hits-stand-up-comedy-1235735117/ |title=Golden Globes Adds Two New Categories: Blockbuster Movies and Stand-Up Comics Enter the Race (Exclusive) |first=Matt |last=Donnelly |work=[[Variety (magazine)|Variety]] |date=September 26, 2023 |access-date=November 26, 2023}}{{cite web |url=https://www.goldderby.com/feature/golden-globes-eliminate-tv-categories-add-box-office-category-1205564400/ |title=Golden Globes eliminate 2 TV categories, add 2 new categories for box office and stand-up performance |first1=Christopher |last1=Tsang |first2=Chris |last2=Beachum |publisher=Gold Derby |date=September 26, 2023 |access-date=November 26, 2023}}{{cite magazine |url=https://ew.com/awards/golden-globes/golden-globes-new-categories-box-office-stand-up-comedian/ |title=Golden Globes adds 2 new awards categories for box office achievements and stand-up comedians |first=Joey |last=Nolfi |magazine=[[Entertainment Weekly]] |date=September 26, 2023 |access-date=November 26, 2023}} ==Winners and nominees== [[File:Baz Luhrmann, Eva Rinaldi (2), Olivia Dejonge, Distributor |- | style="text-align:center"| 3 | ''[[Abbott Elementary]]'' | style="text-align:center"| [[American Broadcasting Company|ABC]] |- | style="text-align:center"| 2 | ''[[The White Lotus]]'' | style="text-align:center"| [[HBO]] |} ===Cecil B. DeMille Award=== The [[Golden Globe Cecil B. DeMille Award|Cecil B. DeMille Award]] is an honorary award for outstanding contributions to the world of entertainment. It is named in honor of its first recipient, director [[Cecil B. DeMille]]. * [[Eddie Murphy]] ===Carol Burnett Award=== The [[Golden Globe Carol Burnett Award|Carol Burnett Award]] is an honorary award for outstanding and lasting contributions to television on or off the screen. It is named in honor of its first recipient, actress [[Carol Burnett]]. * [[Ryan Murphy (producer)|Ryan Murphy]] ==Presenters== {| class="wikitable" |+ ! Name(s) !
